test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: In triangle ABC, the measure of angle A is 34 ° and the measure of angle B is 90 °. What is the measure, in degrees, of angle C?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: Which expression is equivalent to $3xy(2x^{2}+7y)$?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: Which expression is equivalent to $30x^{2}+6x+6$ ？

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: For a particular tree species, it is recommended to plant a maximum of 92 trees per acre. Based on this recommendation, what is the maximum number of trees of this species that can be planted on 2 acres?

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: A gym charges its members a onetime \$34 enrollment fee and a membership fee of \$16 per month. If there are no charges other than the enrollment fee and the membership fee, after how many months will a member have been charged a total of \$178 at the gym?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage:
$f(x)=7x+b$
For the linear function $f$, b is a constant and $f(8)=56$. What is the value of b?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: The function $f$ defined by $f(t)=14t+9$ gives the estimated length, in inches, of a pothos plant t months after Shruti purchased it. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 9 in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Shruti will keep the pothos plant for 9 months.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The pothos plant is expected to grow 9 inches each month.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The pothos plant is expected to grow to a maximum length of 9 inches.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The estimated length of the pothos plant was 9 inches when Shruti purchased it.

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: At a convention center, there are a total of 375 visitors. Each visitor is located in either room A, room B, or room C. If one of these visitors is selected at random, the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room A is 0.48, and the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room B is 0.24. How many visitors are located in room C?

skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage:
$x^{2}-28x=0$
Which of the following is a solution to the given equation?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: A right square pyramid has a height of 12 inches and a volume of 196 cubic inches. What is the perimeter, in inches, of the base of this pyramid?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: The value of a share of stock was \$240 on the day it was purchased. Every year, the value of the share of stock increases by 1 % of its value the previous year. Which function V gives the value, in dollars, of the share of stock t years after the day it was purchased?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: If $f(x)=x+3$ and $g(x)=3x$, what is the value of $6f(3)-g(3)$?

skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables
test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage:
$3x+9y=10$
$rx+18y=20$
In the given system of equations, r is a constant. The system has infinitely many solutions. What is the value of r?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: A club plans to sell tote bags. The club members estimate they will sell 80 tote bags when the bags are priced at \$9 each. For every price increase of \$1, they estimate they will sell 8 fewer bags. What is the estimated revenue, in dollars, when the bags are priced at \$12 each? (revenue = price × number sold)

skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables
test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage:
$3x+6y=17$
$-3x-4y=5$
The solution to the given system of equations is $(x,y)$. What is the value of $2y$?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: What value of w is the solution to the equation $0.7w-0.57=7(w-0.001)$?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: Which quadratic equation has exactly one distinct real solution?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: Quinidra drove her car on a straight road to a certain location. The graph models Quinidra's distance, in miles, from her home as a function of time, in hours. Which of the following could the model describe?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Quinidra drove away from her home at a constant speed for 1 hour, spent 4 hours at the location, then continued to drive away from her home at a constant speed for 1 hour.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Quinidra drove away from her home at a constant speed for 1 hour, spent 4 hours at the location, then drove back to her home at a constant speed for 1 hour.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Quinidra drove away from her home at an increasing speed for 1 hour, spent 4 hours at the location, then drove back to her home at a decreasing speed for 1 hour.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Quinidra drove away from her home at an increasing speed for 1 hour, drove at a constant speed for 4 hours, then drove back to her home at a decreasing speed for 1 hour.

skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: The frequency table summarizes the mass, in grams, of 35 objects. What is the mean mass, in grams, of these objects?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: What is the y -intercept of $y=-(4)^{x}-39$ in the xy -plane?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: A rectangular poster has an area of 330 square inches. A copy of the poster is made in which the length and width of the original poster are each increased by 50 %. What is the area of the copy, in square inches?

test_name: May 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
passage: A circle in the xy -plane has center $(28,5)$ and y -intercepts $(0,-16)$ and $(0,26)$. What is the radius of this circle?
